```json
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Contribution claim and gap statement are present but underdeveloped — the gap ('limits applicability') is asserted rather than argued, and the summary contribution duplicates rather than sharpens the abstract claim; tightening these is central to any meaningful improvement.",
    "R1.2": "The logical chain has gaps: matching and propensity-score weighting are introduced as if sequential, but their relationship is not established; the inference from 'lower estimated bias on IHDP' to 'meaningful contribution to the practical toolkit' is not bridged.",
    "R1.3": "Three evidence-claim tensions are present: (a) 'lower estimated bias' is a proxy metric conflated with actual bias reduction; (b) three IHDP variants are described as sufficient for a generalization claim about 'complex real-world settings'; (c) no comparison against methods other than the classical propensity-score estimator supports the 'meaningful contribution' claim.",
    "R1.4": "Novelty positioning is thin — the delta over Rosenbaum-Rubin (1983) is described only as 'extends with a nonparametric weighting scheme' with no differentiation from the large IPW/CBPS/entropy-balancing literature; improving the draft requires sharpening what is actually new.",
    "R1.6": "Prose quality is a baseline expectation under any reading of 'improve' — sentence-level issues include redundant hedging, weak paragraph transitions, and the final paragraph largely restating the penultimate one."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should improvements target writing quality and clarity only (leaving technical claims and evidence framing intact), or also address substantive issues such as the strength of the evidence-claim alignment and the adequacy of the novelty argument?"
}
```

rationale.R1.1: Contribution claim and gap statement are present but underdeveloped — the gap ('limits applicability') is asserted rather than argued, and the summary contribution duplicates rather than sharpens the abstract claim; tightening these is central to any meaningful improvement.
rationale.R1.2: The logical chain has gaps: matching and propensity-score weighting are introduced as if sequential, but their relationship is not established; the inference from 'lower estimated bias on IHDP' to 'meaningful contribution to the practical toolkit' is not bridged.
rationale.R1.3: Three evidence-claim tensions are present: (a) 'lower estimated bias' is a proxy metric conflated with actual bias reduction; (b) three IHDP variants are described as sufficient for a generalization claim about 'complex real-world settings'; (c) no comparison against methods other than the classical propensity-score estimator supports the 'meaningful contribution' claim.
rationale.R1.4: Novelty positioning is thin — the delta over Rosenbaum-Rubin (1983) is described only as 'extends with a nonparametric weighting scheme' with no differentiation from the large IPW/CBPS/entropy-balancing literature; improving the draft requires sharpening what is actually new.
rationale.R1.6: Prose quality is a baseline expectation under any reading of 'improve' — sentence-level issues include redundant hedging, weak paragraph transitions, and the final paragraph largely restating the penultimate one.

parsed.clarification_question: Should improvements target writing quality and clarity only (leaving technical claims and evidence framing intact), or also address substantive issues such as the strength of the evidence-claim alignment and the adequacy of the novelty argument?
